==Chinese==
{{zh-forms|ss=太|alt=冭,夳,𡙌}}

===Glyph origin===
{{Han etym}}
Originally a {{Han compound|大|廾|水|ls=psc|c1=p|c2=s|c3=s|t2=two hands|t3=water}}. A more conservative variant is [[𡙌]]. The 大 and 廾 later fused into {{zh-l|*𡗗}}. The original meaning according to Shuowen Jiezi was "to slip", perhaps with a reference to seize something wet or to seize a liquid with bare hands. Then it became "too <much>", which was written either as [[太]] or 𡙌 > 泰 or as its variant [[冭]], perhaps due to the influence of its pronunciation and/or the top component of the character, which has a reference to a great quantity. As a result of the changed meaning, it may have been reinterpreted as an {{Han compound|大|廾|水|ls=ic|c1=s|c2=s|c3=s|t1=big; great|t2=two hands|t3=water}}.

===Etymology===
Same word as {{och-l|太|great; greatest; excessive}}; both are derived from {{och-l|大|big}}.
